steps.title: Apply the discriminant shortcut for real solutions
steps.content:
The equation $5x^2 + 10x + 16 = 0$ is quadratic. Rather than solving for $x$, we use the discriminant to determine the number of distinct real solutions:
  • If discriminant $> 0$: two distinct real solutions
  • If discriminant $= 0$: exactly one real solution
  • If discriminant $< 0$: zero real solutions

steps.title: Calculate the discriminant
steps.content:
From $5x^2 + 10x + 16 = 0$, we identify $a = 5$, $b = 10$, and $c = 16$. Compute:
$$10^2 - 4(5)(16) = 100 - 320 = -220$$
Since the discriminant equals $-220 < 0$, the equation has zero distinct real solutions. That matches choice D — D is correct.
